clinical_criteria: At least one non-negated mention from disorder terms (clot, emboli, embolism, phlebitis, thrombi, thromboemboli, thromboembolic, thromboembolism, thrombophlebitis, thromboses, thrombosis, thrombus) AND anatomical site terms (cerebral sinus, inferior vena cava, ivc, pulmonary artery, pulmonary arteries, pulmonary, superior vena cava, svc, transverse sinus, vein, veins, venous) in same or adjacent sentences

At least one non-negated explicit stand-alone term (deep vein thrombosis, deep venous thrombosis, dvt, pe, pulmonary embolism, pulmonary embolus, pulmonary emboli, pulmonary thromboemboli, vein thrombosis, venous thrombosis, venous thromboembolism, vte, inferior vena cava thrombosis, ivc thrombosis, svc thrombosis, superior vena cava thrombosis) without exclusion terms (gonadal vein, ovarian vein, prostatic vein)
Terms must appear in 'sections of interest' within clinical documents
NLP-based negation detection to exclude negated mentions
algorithm_summary: This is a two-step NLP-based phenotyping algorithm for Venous Thromboembolism (VTE). Step 1 uses natural language processing to identify positive VTE cases through either: (1) co-occurrence of disorder-related terms (clot, thrombosis, etc.) with anatomical site terms (vein, pulmonary, etc.) in the same or adjacent sentences, or (2) presence of explicit stand-alone VTE terms (DVT, PE, etc.) without exclusion terms. Step 2 applies ICD-9 exclusion codes - if a patient has negative NLP results but positive ICD-9 codes for VTE history or conditions, they are excluded from the control group. Patients with negative NLP and negative ICD-9 codes become true negative controls. The algorithm prioritizes NLP over structured codes for case identification.
